evidence.title: White House release: President Trump Has Always Been Clear: Iran Cannot Have a Nuclear Weapon
evidence.source_description: Official White House release page captured via web.open on 2026-06-30. Excerpted page text from lines 143-164.
evidence.relevance: Official U.S. statement dated June 17, 2025, shortly after the deadline, showing U.S. position was focused on preventing an Iranian nuclear weapon and referencing peace only conditionally, not confirming a permanent U.S.-Iran peace deal by June 15, 2025.

evidence.title: Times of Oman: Iran announces withdrawal from June 15 talks
evidence.source_description: Times of Oman article opened via web.open on 2026-06-30; captured lines 26-43.
evidence.relevance: Oman-based report dated June 13, 2025 stating Iran announced withdrawal from the sixth round of nuclear negotiations with the United States scheduled for June 15 in Muscat, and that talks were suspended until further notice; adverse to any by-deadline permanent peace deal.

evidence.title: Reuters/Straits Times: June 15 U.S.-Iran nuclear talks cancelled
evidence.source_description: Straits Times page carrying Reuters report, captured via web.open on 2026-06-30. Excerpted page text from lines 39-61.
evidence.relevance: Credible reporting, attributed to Oman and a senior U.S. official, that talks scheduled for June 15, 2025 were cancelled; this is adverse to a claim that a permanent peace deal had been reached by that deadline.

evidence.title: Iran UN Vienna mission statement on U.S. military act of aggression
evidence.source_description: Official Iranian Permanent Mission to UN Vienna page captured via web.open on 2026-06-30. Excerpted page text from lines 76-127.
evidence.relevance: Official Iranian statement dated after the deadline describing U.S. attacks on Iran on June 22, 2025 as aggression and reaffirming Iran's right to self-defense; strongly adverse to existence of a permanent peace deal by June 15, 2025.

evidence.title: White House/Rubio statement: Israel unilateral action against Iran
evidence.source_description: Official White House page opened via web.open on 2026-06-30; captured lines 143-149.
evidence.relevance: Official U.S. statement dated June 12, 2025, shortly before the deadline, describing Israel's unilateral action against Iran, U.S. force-protection posture, and warning Iran not to target U.S. interests; it does not confirm any permanent U.S.-Iran peace deal.

evidence.title: White House fact sheet: Iran refused to reach a deal in June
evidence.source_description: Official White House fact sheet captured via web.open on 2026-06-30. Excerpted page text from lines 143-162.
evidence.relevance: Later official U.S. statement summarizing June 2025 events; it says Iran refused to reach a deal with the United States in June, which is adverse to the proposition and important for weight/provenance analysis.
